Event: Finn learns Puck is father of Quinn’s baby and walks out on glee/football ultimatum (Screw This, I'm Outta Here)
Explanation: Storming out costs him teams and support while leaving others abandoned with little gain.

Event: Finn and Puck decide to team up for revenge on Vocal Adrenaline (Bash Brothers)
Explanation: He gains emotional satisfaction and solidarity with Puck while aiming to harm a rival group.

Event: Finn wishes to be quarterback again; Sam is injured and he is forced to play (Be Careful What You Wish For)
Explanation: His spiteful wish indirectly benefits him by restoring his role while seriously harming Sam.

Event: Finn attacks Jesse for dancing with Rachel
Explanation: Violence over jealousy damages his reputation and relationships without real benefit.

Event: Finn attempts to tip Quinn out of her wheelchair as punishment
Explanation: He endangers an injured person, risks severe backlash, and gets no meaningful gain.

Event: Finn’s premeditated assault on Brody at Santana’s behest
Explanation: The brutal, quasi-enjoyed beating risks serious consequences and only offers fleeting emotional catharsis.

Event: Finn mentors the season 4 newbies through Regionals (Big Brother Mentor)
Explanation: Coaching them boosts his leadership skills and helps them succeed competitively.

Event: Finn prepares new songs and shows up last-minute to save New Directions at Sectionals
Explanation: His initiative secures a performance opportunity and shared success.

Event: Finn accepts quarterback role and popularity before joining glee (Big Man on Campus)
Explanation: Being star QB brings status while giving the school a strong team figurehead.

Event: Finn leads Rachel on romantically to get her to rejoin glee club
Explanation: Manipulating Rachel gets his club back together at real emotional cost to her and some guilt for him.

Event: Finn cheats on Quinn by kissing Rachel and having an emotional affair
Explanation: He gets emotional/romantic gratification while betraying Quinn and destabilizing relationships.

Event: Finn cheats on Rachel by kissing Quinn while Quinn is with Sam
Explanation: He pursues his own happiness explicitly at others’ expense and trashes trust.

Event: Finn harasses his friends to join/perform in glee to keep his popularity
Explanation: He preserves his social standing while pressuring others for his own image.

Event: Finn walks through the school hallway in only underwear to get over confidence issues
Explanation: He accepts short-term embarrassment to address self-esteem; others are mildly entertained or indifferent.

Event: Finn uses Operation: Jealousy—agreeing to sex with Santana partly to make Rachel jealous
Explanation: He gains sexual experience and a hoped-for emotional advantage while manipulating Rachel’s feelings.

Event: Finn lies to Rachel that he’s a virgin when they have sex (Fake Virgin)
Explanation: He secures intimacy under false pretenses, undermining trust if discovered.

Event: Finn tells Will he wants to join the army to be like his (supposed) war-hero father
Explanation: He pursues a self-harming path based on a false ideal that nonetheless honors and emotionally connects to family.

Event: Finn becomes moody and possessive of Rachel due to lack of future plans
Explanation: His possessiveness comforts him but constrains Rachel and strains the relationship.

Event: Finn tries to date Santana and Brittany at once because “girl-on-girl is hot”
Explanation: He seeks sexual/ego gratification while disrespecting both girls and risking fallout.

Event: Finn picks a fight with Jesse at prom over Rachel
Explanation: The altercation briefly vents jealousy but risks social/disciplinary issues and ruins the event for many.

Event: Finn calls Kurt “faggy”
Explanation: The slur damages Kurt and Finn’s own standing, providing no constructive payoff.

Event: Finn outs Santana in the school hallway during an argument
Explanation: Publicly outing her causes major harm and backlash with negligible self-benefit.

Event: Finn tells Sue she has a “retarded baby” during a “Reason You Suck” speech
Explanation: The cruel remark deeply harms and tarnishes him; he instantly regrets it.

Event: Finn cooks Rachel real meat and cake despite knowing she’s vegan and lies about it
Explanation: He gets brief convenience or satisfaction while violating her values and risking trust for no gain.

Event: Finn doesn’t stand up to Karofsky for Kurt in “Furt” (failure to act)
Explanation: His inaction preserves his own safety/status but harms Kurt by omission.

Event: Finn tells Santana to come out of the closet in the hallway, leading to statewide outing
Explanation: His public call-out massively harms her with political/media repercussions and ruins his reputation.

Event: Finn gives Sue a “Reason You Suck” speech (minus the slur) for her behavior
Explanation: Holding Sue accountable can help others while asserting his own moral stance and leadership.

Event: Finn rallies football team and glee club; reforms team to stop bullying
Explanation: He builds cooperation, reduces bullying, and enhances both groups’ cohesion and reputation.

Event: Finn dumps Alpha Bitch Quinn for “loser” Rachel
Explanation: He sacrifices social cachet to pursue a more authentic, caring relationship, helping Rachel’s status and self-worth.

Event: Finn gives a rousing speech at Sectionals about believing in themselves
Explanation: His encouragement boosts morale and performance for both himself and the club.

Event: Finn breaks up with Rachel in “Goodbye” so she won’t be held back
Explanation: He sacrifices his own happiness to free her to pursue her dreams.

Event: Finn loses his virginity to Santana as a rite-of-passage ploy, then regrets it
Explanation: He gains brief status/experience but feels it was a huge mistake and leaves Santana dissatisfied.

Event: Finn kisses Emma to calm her panic attack (“Shut Up” Kiss)
Explanation: He risks his own relationship and gains little while providing Emma some immediate emotional relief.

Event: Finn mentors and helps reform the football team away from bullying
Explanation: He improves team culture and protects vulnerable students while growing into a respected leader.
